title: Schoenus tenuellus
taxonomy: Schoenus tenuellus is a species of sedge, and it belongs to the family Cyperaceae, commonly known as the sedge family. This family is a large group of monocotyledonous flowering plants. Within the Cyperaceae family, Schoenus tenuellus is placed in the genus Schoenus, which includes over 100 species of sedges. The genus Schoenus is further divided into several subgenera, and Schoenus tenuellus is classified under the subgenus Schoenus. The classification of Schoenus tenuellus reflects its close relationships with other sedges within the Cyperaceae family.

intro: Schoenus tenuellus, also known as the slender bog-rush, is a type of sedge that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory and gastrointestinal disorders, as well as wounds and skin irritations.
